title: quercus × morisii
intro: Quercus × morisii, also known as the Japanese white oak, is a hybrid deciduous tree that combines the characteristics of the Japanese evergreen oak (Quercus acutissima) and the Japanese sawtooth oak (Quercus acuta). The bark and leaves of the Quercus × morisii plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Quercus × morisii include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Quercus × morisii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
